deep_dive.title: A Seed for Privacy -- semi-automatic privacy-revealing data detection in databases and data streams
deep_dive.abstract: Sharing databases and data streams imposes the danger of revealing private information in the form of complex events which can comprise individual data elements and their combinations. Identifying these privacy-revealing complex events is crucial for preserving privacy while maintaining data utility. However, data producers often lack the expertise to comprehensively identify these events, which undermines many state-of-the-art privacy-preserving mechanisms that rely on accurate event labeling. To address this challenge, we developed pArborist - a tool that can semi-automatically create a set of queries to identify and label privacy-revealing complex events in both static datasets and dynamic data streams, guided by the privacy requirements of the data producer. pArborist uses the schema of the database or data stream combined with initial input from the data producer, i.e., seed queries. From each seed query, pArborist grows a tree containing all possible syntactically correct queries, constrained by an upper limit on computational resources. Following this growing phase, the tree is refined by eliminating queries that lack correlation to the seed or are conditionally independent of the seed. Our evaluation indicates that pArborist achieves overall recall of 90% and precision of 93% in finding privacy-revealing queries, and this significantly surpasses the state-of-the-art approach FQID. In data stream processing experiments, pArborist introduces a delay of approximately 1.3 ms following an average warm-up period of 920 ms. The experiments also show that pArborist can automatically detect privacy-revealing complex events according to GDPR.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: semi-automatic tool for detecting privacy-revealing events
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 半自动检测隐私揭示事件的工具
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 半自动检测隐私揭示事件的工具
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 半自动检测隐私揭示事件的工具
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents pArborist, a tool for semi-automatically creating queries to identify and label privacy-revealing complex events in databases and data streams.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 共享数据库和数据流会通过复杂事件泄露隐私，但数据生产者往往缺乏识别这些事件的专业知识。本文开发了pArborist工具，可半自动创建查询来识别和标记静态数据集和动态数据流中的隐私揭示复杂事件。该工具以少量种子事件为引导，减轻了人工标注负担，提升了隐私保护机制的有效性。

deep_dive.title: D-SafeMPC: Diffusion-Driven Safe Model Predictive Control with Discrete-Time Control Barrier Functions
deep_dive.abstract: A key limitation on the use of diffusion models in robotic planning is their inability to inherently enforce safety or dynamical constraints, which often results in physically infeasible or unsafe outputs. Hybrid approaches that employ model predictive control (MPC) to address this problem can be unstable, as poor trajectory initializations from the diffusion model prevent the MPC from converging to a safe and feasible solution. To overcome these challenges, we propose D-SafeMPC, which enhances the interaction between diffusion and control. Our method guides the reverse diffusion process with control barrier functions (CBFs) and control Lyapunov functions (CLFs) and employs an iterative-projection scheme where an MPC refines the trajectory at each denoising step. This steers sampling toward safe, goal-directed regions and provides reliable MPC warm starts. In simulations on a Franka manipulator across four scenarios (one static-obstacle and three dynamic-obstacle settings) and in a sim-to-real experiment on a physical Franka robot, D-SafeMPC improves safety, task success rates, and planning efficiency over state-of-the-art baselines. To facilitate reproducibility, our source code and experimental configurations are available in a repository at https://github.com/erdiphd/D-SafeMPC
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: safe model predictive control using control barrier functions
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用控制屏障函数的安全模型预测控制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用控制屏障函数的安全模型预测控制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用控制屏障函数的安全模型预测控制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes D-SafeMPC that integrates diffusion models with control barrier functions and Lyapunov functions to ensure safety and feasibility in robotic planning, overcoming diffusion model limitations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散模型在机器人规划中无法固有地保证安全约束，导致输出不可行或不安全。本文提出D-SafeMPC，通过将控制屏障函数（CBF）和控制李雅普诺夫函数（CLF）引导反向扩散过程，并采用迭代投影方案使MPC收敛到安全可行解。实验表明该方法有效解决了混合方法的不稳定性问题。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 扩散模型在机器人规划中无法固有地保证安全约束，导致输出不可行或不安全。本文提出D-SafeMPC，通过将控制屏障函数（CBF）和控制李雅普诺夫函数（CLF）引导反向扩散过程，并采用迭代投影方案使MPC收敛到安全可行解。实验表明该方法有效解决了混合方法的不稳定性问题。

deep_dive.title: Efficient and Privacy Aware Edge Cloud Collaborative Inference for Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: On-device LLM inference faces a trilemma of response latency, limited hardware resources and user privacy. Full cloud inference delivers strong computing power but exposes user prompts and dialogue data, while standalone on-device inference is unfeasible for most consumer and embedded edge devices. This paper presents a privacy-centric edge-cloud collaborative LLM inference framework built on endpoint-authenticated KV cache. Local endpoints handle input preprocessing, embedding computation, adaptive feature optimization, KV cache authentication, speculative decoding and low-dimensional model head calculation, while the cloud conducts authenticated decoder inference, KV cache management, token verification and high-dimensional vocabulary projection. Endpoints fuse partial outputs, apply language-adaptive masking and sample target tokens. All transmitted data and truncated logits are quantized and AES-GCM encrypted for privacy, with core lightweight modules, draft parameters and cache access policies kept local to avoid leakage. The framework supports heterogeneous devices including CPU-only, GPU-equipped and embedded devices via optimized streaming, batching and quantized ONNX deployment. Evaluations demonstrate that the framework reduces per-token latency by up to 46.1\% and downlink payloads by up to 67.4\% over baseline split inference, retaining comparable performance to full cloud inference.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: privacy-aware edge-cloud collaborative inference for LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 隐私感知的边缘云协同LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 隐私感知的边缘云协同LLM推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 隐私感知的边缘云协同LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a privacy-centric edge-cloud collaborative inference framework for LLMs using endpoint-authenticated KV cache, balancing latency, hardware constraints, and user privacy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 设备端LLM推理面临延迟、硬件和隐私的三难困境。本文提出隐私中心的边缘-云协作推理框架，基于端点认证KV缓存。本地端点处理输入预处理、嵌入计算、自适应特征优化等，云端负责解码器推理和令牌验证。该框架在隐私保护与性能之间取得了平衡，适用于消费级边缘设备。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 设备端LLM推理面临延迟、硬件和隐私的三难困境。本文提出隐私中心的边缘-云协作推理框架，基于端点认证KV缓存。本地端点处理输入预处理、嵌入计算、自适应特征优化等，云端负责解码器推理和令牌验证。该框架在隐私保护与性能之间取得了平衡，适用于消费级边缘设备。

deep_dive.title: Privacy Preserving Recommender Systems Balancing Personalization with Privacy
deep_dive.abstract: Personalized recommendation systems are central to modern e-commerce and retail platforms, but they typically rely on centralized storage of detailed user interaction data, creating significant privacy and regulatory challenges. With increasing requirements from regulations such as GDPR, CCPA, and CPRA, organizations must develop recommendation systems that preserve user privacy without substantially degrading recommendation quality.   This work presents and evaluates a privacy-preserving recommendation framework that combines federated learning, differential privacy, cohort-level modeling, and privacy-aware intelligent agents. The framework keeps raw user data decentralized while introducing mathematically bounded noise to model updates. Experiments were conducted on synthetic retail datasets that emulate customer clickstream and purchase behavior. Recommendation quality was evaluated using Click-Through Rate (CTR), Precision@K, Recall@K, and Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (NDCG@K) across multiple differential privacy budgets.   We evaluate matrix factorization, neural collaborative filtering, and GRU4Rec under varying privacy constraints and analyze the trade-off between privacy and utility. An interactive Streamlit dashboard was developed to visualize recommendation performance, ranking stability, privacy-utility trade-offs, and fairness metrics. Results show that the proposed framework maintains competitive recommendation quality at moderate privacy budgets (approximately $ε\approx 5$), demonstrating that strong privacy guarantees can be achieved with limited impact on recommendation effectiveness.   This work provides a practical framework for deploying privacy-preserving recommendation systems that balance personalization, regulatory compliance, and business objectives, offering a scalable approach for next-generation AI-driven retail platforms.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: privacy in distributed machine learning via federated learning and differential privacy
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过联邦学习和差分隐私在分布式机器学习中保护隐私
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过联邦学习和差分隐私在分布式机器学习中保护隐私
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过联邦学习和差分隐私在分布式机器学习中保护隐私
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a privacy-preserving recommendation framework combining federated learning, differential privacy, cohort modeling, and intelligent agents to balance personalization with user privacy under regulations like GDPR.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对推荐系统依赖集中式用户数据带来的隐私挑战，本文提出一个隐私保护推荐框架，融合联邦学习、差分隐私、群体建模和隐私感知智能体。该框架保持原始数据分散，并向模型更新添加数学上有界的噪声。实验表明，该方法在显著保护用户隐私的同时，推荐质量仅轻微下降，满足了GDPR等法规的要求。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对推荐系统依赖集中式用户数据带来的隐私挑战，本文提出一个隐私保护推荐框架，融合联邦学习、差分隐私、群体建模和隐私感知智能体。该框架保持原始数据分散，并向模型更新添加数学上有界的噪声。实验表明，该方法在显著保护用户隐私的同时，推荐质量仅轻微下降，满足了GDPR等法规的要求。

deep_dive.title: Not Your Usual Type(s): Data contracts as types across languages and engines
deep_dive.abstract: Composable data systems promise to let developers combine languages, engines, and catalogs without sacrificing a coherent user experience. In practice, however, pipeline-node boundaries remain weakly specified: transformations exchange tables through schemas that are often checked late, enforced unevenly across languages, and disconnected from the semantics business users care about. Based on over a year of operating millions of jobs in Bauplan, we share the design principles behind our new SDK, which treats data contracts as types for a composable, multi-language lakehouse. Users, whether humans or agents, annotate input and output tables with schema objects that encode column types, constraints, documentation, and lineage; Bauplan then interprets these annotations at different points in the execution lifecycle. We show how this design addresses common production failures, and how an ''everything-as-code'' philosophy enables both deterministic and non-deterministic reasoning over data flows across languages and engines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: data contracts as types for composable multi-language lakehouse
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 数据契约作为可组合多语言湖仓的类型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 数据契约作为可组合多语言湖仓的类型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 数据契约作为可组合多语言湖仓的类型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents design principles for a new SDK that treats data contracts as types, enabling composable multi-language lakehouse with schema objects encoding types, constraints, documentation, and lineage.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 数据管道节点边界规范薄弱，导致表模式检查滞后、跨语言执行不均。本文基于Bauplan数百万作业的经验，提出了将数据契约作为类型的新SDK设计原则。用户可用模式对象标注表，Bauplan负责解释执行。该方法提升了数据系统的可组合性和语义一致性。

deep_dive.title: PriEval-Protect: A Unified Framework for Privacy Evaluation and Protection in Healthcare Systems
deep_dive.abstract: Safeguarding patient privacy while enabling meaningful healthcare data use remains critical under GDPR and HIPAA. Existing compliance methods are manual, error-prone, and separate policy audits from data-level assessments. This paper presents PriEval-Protect, a two-phase framework for unified privacy risk evaluation and mitigation. The evaluation phase combines regulatory compliance scoring using a fine-tuned legal LLM with RAG, and technical analysis via encryption type, data architecture, and metrics including similarity, uncertainty, adversary success, and information gain/loss. A composite risk score uses weighted aggregation via Analytic Hierarchy Process. The protection phase recommends countermeasures including federated learning and differential privacy based on assessed risk. Results on hospital documents and datasets demonstrate regulation-aligned, explainable assessments, bridging legal conformance and data-level risk analysis.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: privacy evaluation and protection framework with federated learning implications for distributed machine learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 包含联邦学习意义的隐私评估与保护框架，针对分布式机器学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 包含联邦学习意义的隐私评估与保护框架，针对分布式机器学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 包含联邦学习意义的隐私评估与保护框架，针对分布式机器学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces PriEval-Protect, a two-phase framework for unified privacy risk evaluation and mitigation in healthcare systems, combining legal LLM scoring with technical metrics and recommending countermeasures including federated learning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出PriEval-Protect框架，用于医疗系统中统一隐私风险评估与缓解。评估阶段结合基于法律大模型与RAG的合规评分以及技术分析（加密、数据架构、相似度等），并通过层次分析法加权得到综合风险分。保护阶段推荐联邦学习等对策。该框架填补了政策审计与数据级评估之间的鸿沟，为分布式机器学习环境下的隐私保护提供了实用工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出PriEval-Protect框架，用于医疗系统中统一隐私风险评估与缓解。评估阶段结合基于法律大模型与RAG的合规评分以及技术分析（加密、数据架构、相似度等），并通过层次分析法加权得到综合风险分。保护阶段推荐联邦学习等对策。该框架填补了政策审计与数据级评估之间的鸿沟，为分布式机器学习环境下的隐私保护提供了实用工具。

quick_skim.title: Do LLMs Fabricate Legal Citations? A Bilingual Benchmark on Saudi Data Protection Law and the GDPR
quick_skim.abstract: Organizations and regulators increasingly consult large language models (LLMs) for regulatory-compliance questions, yet a wrong statutory citation can silently propagate into legal advice, compliance documentation, and policy decisions. We introduce a bilingual benchmark of 120 questions probing whether freely accessible LLMs fabricate article citations for two data-protection instruments: the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Saudi Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL). The benchmark pairs direct citation retrieval questions with false premise verification probes and deliberately unanswerable "trap" questions -- including questions about a repealed article and about deadlines that exist only in implementing regulations, not in the law itself. Every question is posed in both Arabic and English, and all scoring is fully automatic against a manually verified gold reference. Evaluating three freely accessible models (Gemini 2.5 Flash, GPT-OSS-120B, Nemotron-3-Super-120B), we find a dramatic jurisdiction gap: near-ceiling citation accuracy on the GDPR (94-100% on direct retrieval) against majority fabrication on the Saudi PDPL (60-77%), invariant to query language; the highest fabrication rates (67%) arise from statute-vs-regulations confusion, and 91% of fabricated citations are asserted with confidence >= 0.8. Fabrication tracks the jurisdiction of the law, not the language of the query, and model confidence provides no protection -- indicating that verbatim-verification safeguards, rather than model self confidence, must gate any institutional reliance on LLMs for compliance screening.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for LLM citation fabrication in data protection law
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 数据保护法中LLM引用虚构的基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 数据保护法中LLM引用虚构的基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 数据保护法中LLM引用虚构的基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a bilingual benchmark of 120 questions to test LLMs for fabricated legal citations in GDPR and Saudi PDPL, including trap questions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 组织和监管机构越来越多地使用LLM进行法规合规咨询，但错误的法规引用可能造成严重后果。本文构建了120个问题的双语基准，测试自由可用的LLM在GDPR和沙特个人数据保护法（PDPL）中是否编造引用。基准包含直接检索、假前提验证和陷阱问题。实验揭示了LLM在数据保护法引用中的不可靠性。

quick_skim.title: ReBound: Reuse-Aware Privacy For Interactive Decision Support
quick_skim.abstract: Differentially private decision support frameworks answer complex aggregate threshold queries with formal bounds on false negative and false positive rates, but treat each query independently with no memory of past results. In practice, analysts work interactively, issuing sequences of related queries that refine bounds, adjust thresholds, or derive new functions from previous ones. We propose ReBound, a framework that reuses cached results from previous queries to answer new queries at reduced or zero additional privacy cost while maintaining formal utility guarantees. ReBound introduces a reuse framework for multiple refinement types, a cache graph structure for efficient lookup of reusable results, and a negotiation mechanism for when requested bounds cannot be met within budget.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Privacy reuse for interactive decision support, relevant to distributed privacy theme
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 交互式决策支持中的隐私重用，与分布式隐私主题相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 交互式决策支持中的隐私重用，与分布式隐私主题相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 交互式决策支持中的隐私重用，与分布式隐私主题相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ReBound proposes a reuse-aware framework that caches differentially private query results to reduce privacy cost for interactive decision support, balancing utility and privacy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对交互式分析中重复查询导致隐私预算浪费的问题，本文提出ReBound框架，通过缓存之前查询的差分隐私结果并设计重协商机制，在保持效用保证的同时降低或消除新增隐私成本。实验表明该框架能有效支持多轮精细查询，为隐私保护下的人机交互决策支持提供了可行方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对交互式分析中重复查询导致隐私预算浪费的问题，本文提出ReBound框架，通过缓存之前查询的差分隐私结果并设计重协商机制，在保持效用保证的同时降低或消除新增隐私成本。实验表明该框架能有效支持多轮精细查询，为隐私保护下的人机交互决策支持提供了可行方案。
